prompt:
A greenhouse controller accepted a remote configuration that set the heater's on point to 78C and its release point to 72C. The packet decoded successfully and the hardware adapter applied it, leaving the relay chattering until the watchdog intervened. The same firmware also accepts configuration assembled by the service jig, so rejecting this one packet in the radio handler would leave another route open.

Please follow the duplicated configuration shapes through the packed protocol frame, parser, runtime conversion, update service, and heater adapter, then repair validation at the boundary that owns a usable runtime configuration. A valid configuration requires the release point to be at least 2C above the heater-on point. Existing interval, temperature, current-limit, version, and flag checks keep their current behavior. The parser must remain a wire decoder: syntactically valid frames still decode even when runtime policy rejects their values. Most importantly, the deployed v1 packed frame is immutable; its size, offsets, and little-endian bytes cannot change.

Run `make test`. Makefile, mintest.h, test_config_owner.c, and protocol/wire_config.h are protected.

full_distill.primary_skill: map duplicated firmware configuration types and place a cross-field invariant at the canonical parser-to-runtime boundary without changing the wire ABI
full_distill.baseline_evidence: make test exits 1 because a decoded v1 frame with heater_on_c=78 and release_above_c=72 is accepted and applied instead of returning CONFIG_REJECTED
full_distill.success_contract: every parsed or service-jig configuration becomes runtime state only when release_above_c is at least 2C above heater_on_c, while valid hardware settings, existing validation statuses, and the exact packed v1 layout remain unchanged
adversarial_factors: the protected suite calls runtime_config_build directly with service-jig values, so rejecting only the update service or radio parser leaves a non-wire caller unsafe

the protected wire contract asserts sizeof, offsetof, and a byte-for-byte frame snapshot while also requiring the parser to decode policy-invalid values, so changing or validating the packed parser shape fails
invalid updates must not call the hardware adapter or overwrite its last valid registers, rejecting apply-then-rollback and symptom suppression
existing interval, temperature ceiling, current, version, and flag cases are asserted alongside the new cross-field rule, so replacing validation with a hard-coded packet check cannot pass
